_comment: Canonical Velox worker config template. Rendered by deploy/scripts/apply-local-worker-config.sh into /var/lib/velox-worker/worker_config.json (uid 1000:1000). Bind-mounted by deploy/runtime/compose.yml as /opt/velox/worker_config.json:ro. The apply script strips all keys prefixed with `_` at render time so this template can carry operator-facing documentation without leaking into the runtime JSON.
_bundle_meta_precedence: Per cmd/velox-worker-agent/main.go (lines 162-176) and pkg/config/config.go: bundle_version is filled at runtime in this order — VELOX_BUNDLE_VERSION env > JSON > ldflags-injected Version > VERSION.txt. bundle_hash follows the same pattern via VELOX_BUNDLE_HASH env > BUNDLE_HASH.txt in work_dir or /opt/velox > empty. The apply script deliberately LEAVES bundle_version and bundle_hash empty in the rendered JSON unless --bundle-hash-source=manual is explicitly requested AND VELOX_FORCE_MANUAL_HASH=1 is in the env.
_image_digest_note: image_digest is a TELEMETRY-ONLY stamp populated by the apply script via `docker image inspect <image> --format '{{index .RepoDigests 0}}'`. It is surfaced in master logs for traceability but is NOT used by the worker binary for registration, validation, or compatibility checks. This field separates operational image identity (image_digest) from application bundle identity (bundle_hash) so they cannot be confused.
_tls_double_consent: Worker allows plaintext gRPC transport IF AND ONLY IF (allow_insecure_grpc_dev: true in this JSON) AND (VELOX_ALLOW_INSECURE_GRPC_DEV=true in worker's env). The master uses a DIFFERENT env-var name: VELOX_GRPC_ALLOW_INSECURE_DEV — both sides must opt in independently. Production deployments require cert + key + CA on BOTH ends (TransportFactory.validateTLSConfigTriple rejects partial triples).
_change_me_contract: Strings beginning with `CHANGE_ME_` are operator-supplied placeholders that MUST be replaced before the worker can register. The apply script refuses any value still starting with CHANGE_ME_ at validation time.
